sense.definition: a type of cycling that involves riding on unpaved roads and trails using a bike designed for rougher terrain

examples.x: Gravel cycling offers a thrilling escape from paved roads.
Many cyclists are switching to gravel cycling for its adventure appeal.
The new gravel cycling event attracted hundreds of participants.
She invested in a durable bike specifically for gravel cycling.
Gravel cycling routes often feature beautiful, remote landscapes.

sense.definition: a competitive cycling event on roads for athletes with physical disabilities

examples.x: The para cycling road race will take place downtown this Sunday.
She trained for months to compete in the para cycling road championship.

sense.definition: the sport or activity of riding a bicycle

examples.x: Cycling is an eco-friendly mode of transportation that helps reduce carbon emissions.
The annual cycling event attracted participants from all over the country.
Many people find cycling to be a fun way to socialize while exercising with friends.

sense.definition: to propel and operate a bicycle or other pedal-powered vehicle

examples.x: Every morning, she pedals her bike to work, enjoying the fresh air and exercise on her commute.
The children eagerly pedaled their bicycles down the winding path in the park
In the park, families can be seen pedaling tandem bicycles.
On weekends, they love to pedal their bikes through scenic trails.
During the cycling race, the athlete had to pedal vigorously to maintain a competitive pace.
In spinning class, participants were instructed to pedal at different intensities to simulate various terrains.

sense.definition: a sport in which participants use specially designed bicycles to perform tricks and race on a dirt track

examples.x: He started training for bicycle motocross at a young age.
The bicycle motocross competition drew large crowds last weekend.
Bicycle motocross requires both skill and speed on the track.
She won a gold medal in the international bicycle motocross event.

sense.definition: a competitive sport where cyclists race on specially designed oval tracks

examples.x: She won gold in the track cycling event at the Olympics.
He set a new world record in track cycling last year.
The track cycling team practiced their sprints and strategies.
The national track cycling trials will be held next weekend.
Track cycling bikes are lightweight and have no brakes.

sense.definition: a competitive sport where cyclists race on paved roads over various distances and terrains

examples.x: The Tour de France is the most famous road bicycle racing event.
She trains daily to prepare for road bicycle racing competitions.
She switched from mountain biking to road bicycle racing last year.
The new bike model is designed specifically for road bicycle racing.

sense.definition: the activity or sport of riding a mountain bike over rough ground

examples.x: Mountain biking is a great way to explore the outdoors.
He bought a new bike for mountain biking last weekend.
The park has trails specifically designed for mountain biking.
Mountain biking requires skill and proper equipment.
Beginners often start mountain biking on easier trails.

sense.definition: a form of bicycle racing consisting of many laps on a short course featuring various terrains and obstacles

examples.x: Cyclo-cross races often involve steep hills and muddy paths.
The popularity of cyclo-cross has been growing rapidly in recent years.
She trained all summer to compete in her first cyclo-cross race.
During a cyclo-cross event, riders must frequently dismount and carry their bikes over barriers.
Cyclo-cross courses are known for their challenging and unpredictable conditions.

sense.definition: a form of bicycle racing on short, oval tracks

examples.x: The local club is hosting a cycle speedway event this weekend.
He trained rigorously for months to compete in the cycle speedway championship.
Cycle speedway requires both speed and strategic maneuvering.
She won her first cycle speedway race at the age of ten.
Cycle speedway riders need to have excellent bike-handling skills.

sense.definition: a formation in cycling where riders follow closely behind one another to reduce wind resistance and conserve energy

examples.x: The team formed a paceline to maintain a steady pace against the headwind.
Riding in a paceline, cyclists take turns leading to share the workload.
Joining the paceline, he felt the reduced drag immediately.
She tucked into the paceline to conserve energy for the final sprint.
He struggled to stay in the paceline, fighting fatigue.

sense.definition: the technique of closely following another vehicle or cyclist in races to reduce wind resistance and conserve energy

examples.x: Drafting is a key strategy in conserving energy during long rides.

sense.definition: a cycling maneuver to extend the front wheel at the finish line for a close win

examples.x: His bike throw was so precise that it earned him the victory.
She won the race by a fraction of a second with a perfect bike throw.
She practiced her bike throw daily to improve her sprint finishes.
In the final sprint, his bike throw made all the difference.
Her bike throw technique impressed everyone at the championships.

sense.definition: an intense burst of speed used in cycling to finish a race or overtake an opponent

examples.x: She saved her energy for a powerful sprint at the finish line.
The final sprint decided the winner of the race.
She dominated the sprint stage of the cycling tour.
His explosive sprint left his competitors far behind.
The sprint to the finish was thrilling to watch.

sense.definition: a technique in cycling where the cyclist pedals while standing on the pedals, usually during ascents or steep sections of the route

examples.x: The cyclist's cadence increased as he transitioned into a standing climb.
The cyclist's powerful legs allowed him to maintain speed during a standing climb.
He shifted to a standing climb to tackle the hill more effectively.
In mountainous terrain, experienced cyclists utilize standing climbs to conserve energy.
During the race, she preferred using a standing climb technique on inclines.

sense.definition: a maneuver in cycling where one rider accelerates to pave the way for a teammate's sprint

examples.x: He performed a perfect leadout for his teammate.
She surged ahead after a strong leadout.

sense.definition: a technique in cycling where the cyclist climbs hills or inclines while remaining seated on a bicycle

examples.x: Seated climbing allows for better aerodynamics compared to standing.
He maintained a steady pace during the seated climbing portion of the race.
Seated climbing can be less taxing on the muscles than standing.
Seated climbing requires good endurance and leg strength.
During the seated climbing drills, he focused on his cadence.
